name: Punch Drunk
desc: Extra Headshot Damage
notes: "If the first shot doesn't drop 'em, the second one will. Or the third, third's usually good."

name: Nightwatcher
desc: Scanner Pulse Haste
notes: "Through glass and steel; looking at you; a view so cruel."

name: Chill Out
desc: Has 2 Mod Slots and only 1 Minor Stat
notes: "Revenge is a dish best served cold."- Someone who remembers you

name: Visionario
desc: Extra optimal range
notes: "So far be distant; and good night, sweet friend" - A Midsummer Night's Dream

name: The Hollow Man
desc: Damage to Health
notes: "No whimper. No bang. Just silence. The end of all things."

name: Liquid Engineer
desc: Killing an enemy adds and refreshes a stack of +12% bonus armor for 10s. Max stack is 10
notes: "Is that oil, or blood?" -BTSU Combat Medic

name: Hermano
desc: You and allies within 15m of your deployed skills, gain +30% reload speed and reduces active cooldowns by 0.6 each second.

name: The Courier
desc:
When you apply a status effect, it is also applied to all enemies within 10m of your target. 

Cooldown: 10s
notes: "May this carry your soul to paradise" -The Priest

name: Backbone
desc:
Killing an enemy increases total weapon damage by 7% for 15s.
Stacks up to 5.
Grenade kills add 2 stacks.
notes: "Your first kill is hard. But it gets easier the more you do it." -The Stratagist

name: Anarchist's Cookbook
desc: Applying a status effect increases total weapon damage by 18% for 27s
notes: "Conley, I think you'll find some great toys in here. Try not to blow everything up while you're looking through it." - Aaron Keener

name: Strategic Alignment
desc: Applying a status effect to an enemy increases total skill damage and repair by 20% for 27s
notes: "I know this is going to look liike we're destroying everything. Don't worry about it. We don't make mistakes. Just happy accidents." - Bob Ross

name: Lavoisier
desc: Applying Blind, Ensnare, Confuse, or Shock to an enemy grants 50% of your armor as bonus armor to you and all allies within 30 meters of that enemy for 10 seconds.
notes: "A piece of perfection, if you understand what you can do with it."  Anonymous

name: Matador
desc:
When you are within 10m of an enemy, gain 23% bonus armor for 5s. Stacks up to 3 times.

Cooldown: 5s
notes: "I used to dance with death itself, but grew tired of the performance. I think I prefer having the odds stacked against me instead." - The Bull

name: The Gift
desc:
Increases total weapon damage by 25%. 
Taking damage disables buff for 3s
notes: "Only after everything has been taken, can you learn to give. When the vessel is full, the soul is empty." - Unknown

name: Percussive Maintenance
desc: Skill kills increase total skill damage by 25% for 27s
notes:
"Have you tried turning it off and on again?"
(note: don't turn your devices on and off. Use the Restart function to solve problems instead. They have different functions.)

name: Vigil
desc:
Amplifies total weapon damage for 10s when swapping between your primary and secondary weapons if they are different.

45% to enemies within 15m for Shotguns and SMG's.
45% to enemies further than 25m for Rifles and MMR's.
20% to enemies between 15-25m for LMG's and AR's
At most once per 5s per weapon type.
notes: "Honor the fallen."

name: Devil's Due
desc:
When you are below 20% armor, critical hits repair 2.5% of missing armour. 
On kill, critical hits repair 2.5% of missing armor for 4 seconds.
Lowest possible repair is 0% armour

Each red core attribute adds 1 second to the time.
Maximum is 10 seconds.
notes:
When he comes calling
It's best not be stalling
Or you might find yourself falling
Down, down, down below
- Inscription inside the pack

name: Battery Pack
desc: Kills from cover reduce skill cooldowns by 15%.

name: The Setup
desc: Enemies you hit with shotguns and marksman rifles amplifies the damage they take by 15% from all sources for 5s.

name: Force Multiplier
desc: Shooting an enemy increases total skill damage by 30% for 3s.
notes: "You've got to think about force multipliers in this line of work. You don't always have backup, so make use of what's available." - SHD Training Guide

name: Proxy
desc:
Enemies that walk within 3 meters of your Hive, Turret, Remote Pulse or Decoy are shocked.

Arm time: 2 seconds. Cooldown per Skill: 8 seconds
notes: "Hostile detected. Hostile Eliminated." -Alexander Ortiz

name: Axel
desc:
Using an armor kit grants +1 Skill Tier for 15s. 
If already at Skill Tier 6, grants Overcharge. 

Cooldown 30s.
notes: "Don't fear your instincts, Society taught you how to fail."

name: Cap'n
desc:
Performing a cover to cover grants 20% of your armor as bonus armor to you and all allies for 10s. 
This is Tripled if you end within 10m of an enemy. 

Cooldown: 10s.
notes: "Get behind me."

name: Festive Delivery
desc:
Concussion grenades become Festive Grenades.

Hitting an enemy with a Festive Grenade will cause explosions to happen around two other enemies within 20m. 10m in pvp 

*Dealing 15% damage in PVP
notes: "You get fireworks, and you get fireworks. Everybody gets fireworks!"

name: Henri
desc: While you are within 10m of an ally or skill, total weapon damage is increased by 20%
notes: "We live together, we learn together, we die together. But, first we make each other stronger."  Anonymous

name: Hunter-Killer
desc:
While you have bonus armour, gain 3 stacks each second up to a max of 10. Each stack increases total weapon damage by 4% to enemies within 10m.

All stacks are lost when you have no bonus armour.
notes:
"These guys came out of nowhere. Dressed to kill and armed to the teeth. Turns out, we were the ones they were sent to kill." 
- Division Agent

name: Everyday Carrier
desc:
Using an armor kit has a 75% chance to not consume the armor kit. Specialization armor kit bonuses are increased by 100%.

Requires 10 seconds to arm talent
notes:
"What's in my everyday carry? As many armour plates as I can fit" 

- Agent Sanders

name: Chainkiller
desc:
After killing an enemy with a headshot, your next weapon hit within 30s deals 150% of that killing blow’s damage in addition to it.

Damage is capped to 800% of your weapon damage. This is raised to 1250% if your headshot damage is greater than 150%.
notes:
"Ninety-nine bottles of beer on the wall, ninety-nine bottles of beer. Shoot one down, watch it explode, ninety-eight bottles of beer on the wall..."

- Bored True Sons Marksman

name: Pointman
desc:
Deploying a shield makes it invulnerable for 5s and grants 50% of your armor as bonus armor to all other allies for 20s. 

Cooldown: 25s
notes:
"Knuckles, Ron, you're with me.  Duchess, you're on point. Here, take this. Maybe it'll save yer ass." 
- James Dragov

name: Vedmedytsya Vest
desc: While in cover weapon handling is increased by +50%
notes: Brace yourself. Stand firm against the tide of history.

name: The Sacrifice
desc:
All damage you deal is amplified by 30%. 
All damage you take is amplified by 60%.
notes: "Only after everything has been given, can you expect to receive. When the vessel is empty, the soul is complete."

name: Zero F’s
desc:
When your armor is depleted, repair 100% of your armor. 
Cooldown: 55s
notes: "Behold! The field in which I grow my fucks. Lay thine eyes upon it and see that it is barren."

name: Ferocious Calm
desc: After staying in cover for 8 seconds, increase your and all allies’ total weapon and skill damage by 14% as long as you remain in cover or in a cover-to-cover move.
notes: "The invention of the gun required a redefinition of courage from aggressive ferocity to calmness under fire" - Robert L . O'Connell

name: Door-Kicker’s Knock
desc: Damaging an enemy with a skill increases total weapon damage by 18% for 20 seconds.
notes: "Just drop your seeker and let it go. Don't let the bang trip you up. You've got to prepare for the shock." - SHD Training Guide

name: Caesar's Guard
desc: Skill kills have a 30% chance to reset skill cooldowns

name: Closer
desc: Amplifies total weapon and skill damage by 20% to pulsed enemies.

name: Cherished
desc: Applies blind to enemy hit in the head. Cooldown 20 seconds. Applies bleed to an enemy hit in the chest. Cooldown 20 seconds
notes: "My gift to you, to protect your sacred heart." The Priest

name: Carpenter
desc:
Grenade Radius is increased by 75%
Grenade that kill an enemy are refunded.
Grenades can now be ''cooked'' by holding the fire button, making them explode earlier.
Gain 15% bonus armor while aiming grendes
notes: "The things i break stay broken." - The Stratagist

name: Bober
desc: If you are below 30% Armor, headshots from cover repair 30% of your armor. Cooldown 2 seconds.
notes: "No way they can get me in this." - Diesel

name: Pristine Example
desc: Increases total weapon damage by 6% every second you are aiming while scoped 8x or higher, up to 60%.
notes: "Only the first shot is clean. Everything after that is just messy."

name: Sleight
desc:
Grants +40% bonus armor while reloading.

Grants 0-30% of your armor as bonus armor to all other allies when they are reloading, based on your Blue core attribute.
notes: "I'm feeling generous."

name: Combustor
desc:
Whenever you throw a skill, 1.5 seconds after landing, it creates an explosion damaging enemies within 5m. 
Afterwards explosions will occur evey 5s.

Damage scales with skill tier dealing 25-100% damage of a concussion grenade. 

Occurs once per skill. 

Applies to Remote Pulse, All Turrets, All Hives, Explosive Seeker, Cluster Seeker, Mender Seeker, Decoy, All Traps.
notes: "My operatives would never be left alone in the field" - Natalya Sokolova

name: Equalizer
desc: Critical hits increase total weapon damage by 1% for 10s. Stacks up to 24 times.

name: Deathgrips
desc: Armor on Kill
notes:
"Most people are white-knuckling their way through life, sterss levels off the charts. Working dead-end jobs paying off their loans, student debts and fucking lattes. 
That shit will kill you without clarity in your life, and nothing brings clarity like hanging off the side of a mountain."

name: Contractor’s Gloves
desc: Damage to Armor
notes: "Never met a man I couldn't break. Apply enough pressure on a small enough point, and anything cracks."

name: Motherly Love
desc: Skill Health
notes: "Life is horrible. Once you realise that, it's easier to appreciate the moments that are the exceptions. Makes it all a bit more realistic to endure."

name: Eagles Grasp
desc: Weapon Handling
notes:
"Just wait until I get my hands on them." 
- Saint

name: Firm Handshake
desc: Extra Status effect
notes: "You can tell a lot about a person from their handshake. It's also a great pathogenic delivery system."

name: Forge
desc: Shield Health
notes:
"The space in between is where you want to be. Not the hammer. Not the anvil. You are the impact, 
the energy, the potential. Bend or break, you will find purpose. And in purpose, there is truth."

name: Ammo Dump
desc: Ammo Capacity
notes:
"Whaddya mean yer outta ammo? Dig around in that mess you call a holster and find something to shove in your gun and shoot!" 
- True Sons platoon leader

name: Picaros Holster
desc: Weapon Damage Minor Stat

name: Spot-On
desc: Accuracy
notes: "It's all in the hip."

name: Salvo
desc: Rate of Fire

name: Claws Out
desc: Melee Damage + Pistol Damage
notes: "Rip and tear!"

name: Fox’s Prayer
desc: Damage to Targets Out of Cover
notes: "Ever heard of an atheist in a foxhole? Me neither."

name: Snow Machine
desc: status effects apply snowfall to target
notes: "Like our friends down under, if we want snow on Christmas we have to make it ourselves"

name: Turmoil
desc: When dealing Weapon Damage to an enemy, 50% of the damage will be dealt to another enemy within 30m instead. When there are no enemies nearby, deal damage normally. Does not apply to Armor Plates
notes: Never let them know your next move.

name: Emperor’s Guard
desc: % Armor Regen
notes: "Fall down seven times, get up eight."

name: Grease
desc: Extra Status effect
notes: "I'm not in danger, you're in danger"

name: Cloak
desc: % Reduced Threat
notes: "No sudden movements."

name: Vile
desc:
Status effects also apply a damage over time debuff for 10s.  

Total Damage dealt is equal to 50% of your concussion grenade damage and increased by your status effect attributes.
notes:
"You cling to life like a rebellious teen resisting curfew. But death is patient... and so am I." 

- Dr Vilis, BTSU Physician

name: Catharsis
desc: Taking damage builds stacks to a cap of 30. Each stack grants 1.5% Weapon Damage. Taking damage at max stacks triggers a purge, removing all stacks and Status Effects and then dropping a healing cloud which restores 5% of Max Armor for 10s to all allies in the cloud.
notes:
"The establishment does everything in its power to ensure that revolutionary rage is redirected into empty outlets which provide pressure releases for 
desires that could become dangerous if allowed to progress" 

-George L. Jackson

name: Coyote’s Mask
desc:
You and all allies gain a bonus based on the distance of the last enemy you hit.

0-15m: +25% critical hit damage
15-25m: +10% critical hit chance and +10% critical hit damage
25m+: +25% critcal hit Chance
notes:
"Lived through this bullet. I'll live through yours." 
- Coyote

- Do not bother farming for this exotic through Coyote at Jefferson Trade Centre. Instead, farm Countdown with mask targeted loot. It is more efficient.
- The Jefferson Trade Centre mission becomes tragic when you realise that Espinoza is one of Agent Kelso's few trusted friends in DC.
- Agent Kelso was overly confident that Coyote wouldn't kill Espinoza not because she didn't care, but because she was in denial and couldn't believe
that anything bad could happen to him.
- Kelso and Espinoza's backstory can be heard on the Embassy classified assignment collectibles. Click on this cell to watch a video of that mission.
-A group member can receive all 3 damage buffs at the same time, However, a group member can only have 1 of each damage buff at a time
(e.g. if there are 2 CQC players, you will still only get +25% critical hit damage)

name: Catalyst
desc:
Catalysis
Dealing and recieving status effects (Burn, Bleed, Shock, EMP/Disrupt, Poison and blind/disorient) builds stacks of Catalysis to a maximum of 12. each stack grants:
+2% Weapon Damage
+2% Status Effects

Each enemy within 25 Meters that is affected by a status effect grants +1 stack/sec. Being affectedby status effects grants +2 stacks/sec. 
Stacks decay at 1 stac/sec after 5s if no enemies in the radius or you are affected by status effects.

Reactant
At max stacks, killing a status-affected enemy grants bonuses for 5s:
+25% bonus armor
+20% reload speed

Stabilizer
While burning, you can maintain ADS without disruption.
notes:
"We're still being tested. The only constant variable is fire"


-the status effect that give stacks do not include foam

name: Tinkerer
desc:
If your Primary and Secondary weapon are not Exotic or Named and of the same type, the Secondary Weapon's talent will also be applied to the Primary Weapon.

Doesn't apply to talents that include Weapon Swapping.
notes: "Why not both."

name: Acosta's Go-Bag
talent:
One in the Hand...




...Two in the bag
desc:
Throwing a grenade refunds it and grants +1 skill tier for 15s. 
Grants overcharge if already at skill tier 6

Cooldown: 60s

+1 Armor Kit Capacity
+3 Grenade Capacity
+25% Ammo Capacity
+10% Repair-Skills
+10% Status Effects
notes:
"You never know what you're going to need, so it's best to be prepared." 
- Eva Acosta, Urban Spelunker

- This backpack can drop with a variety of strap colours, namely red, green and purple. Gotta collect them all!
- Eva Acosta is a character from Division 1 who left audio logs all over the city detailing her exploration. Due to the nature of what she sees, these logs get 
increasingly disturbing. Click on this cell to listen to Eva's logs.
- Eva does not seem to be associated with any organsation, and even her spelunking crew seems to be a group of friends. It is evident, however, that she has
lived in New York all her life and cares deeply about well being of the city. For this reason, I chose to use a symbol from a redesign of the New York flag.
- The full redesigned New York flag is from r/Vexillology

name: Birdie's Quick Fix Pack
desc:
+80% Revive Speed
-50% Weakened Debuff timer
+45% Hive efficiency
Provides +50% damage resistance to both agents while reviving or being revived, and for 5s after a successful Hive Revive, or 10s after a Manual Revive. Any successful revives provide  +1 Skill tier for 5s. Revives at skill tier 6 grant Overcharge for 15s.
notes: "Get up, we're out of body bags."

name: Memento
desc:
Enemies you kill drop a trophy on death. Collecting trophies provides both a short and long term buff, the first of which scales with the number of core attributes equipped and lasts 10s.

Red Core - 5% Weapon Damage
Blue Core - 10% Bonus Armor
Yellow Core - 5% Skill Effciency

For every trophy collected gain an additional +1% weapon damage, +1% skill efficiency and +0.1% armor regeneration for 300s

Maximum 30 stacks
notes:
"I took from those who watched, hunted, and collected, until only tgheir shadows remained. Now my watch has ended, so yours may begin."

- Inscription by an unknown author


- The name "Memento" is a reference to  the fact that Hunters keep watches of Agents they have killed as sick mementos. 
- The axes on the backpack are Hunter axes. These Crash Axes, the same ones found in planes. While usually used for breaching and rescue, 
Hunters use these axes to melee Agents who get too close.
- +5% Skill Efficiency grants +5% of every yellow minor attribute (skill haste, status effects, skill health...). +1% Skill Efficiency grants +1%.
- Memento is suspiciously similar to Prism Gloves, a joke exotic posted by Azurmen32 to the Division 2 subreddit. This is part of a running gag about 
rainbow roll items being good.

name:
NinjaBike 
Messenger Backpack
desc:
Slots in with any equipped Gear Set and/or 
Brand Set item to fulfill a requirement towards 
unlocking a Gear and/or Brand Sets bonus. 
Can unlock bonuses from multiple sets 
simultaneously.
notes: "From the depths of hell, this has been resurrected as if it cannot die." The Priest

name: Collector
desc:
+3 Grenade Capacity
+50% Grenade Radius
+35% Grenade Damage
+25% Grenade Damage for each extra enemy caught in the blast
Automatically regenerate grenades every 30s, if you have less than 2 grenades in your inventory.
notes:
"Let's see the Division glue you back together after this."
- The Strategist

name: Provocator
desc: Recieve +25% Damage resistance from enemies within 20m
notes: "One does not simply wear red into battle. A red knight is not afraid but ready to provoke ." - Anonymous

name: Beacon
desc:
Both you and Allies receive +30% Critical Hit Damage, +15% Skill Efficiency and +2% Armor Regen when an Ally is within 10 m of you.

Receive +15% Critical Hit Damage, +7.5% Skill Efficiency and +1% Armor Regen when one of your Skills is within 10m of you.

Only the highest group of buffs can be active at one time.
notes: With Me.

name:
Tardigrade 
Armor System
desc:
Whenever you or any ally's armor breaks, they gain 80% of your armor as bonus armor for 10s.

Cooldown per ally: 45s

Killing an enemy with your specialization weapon removes this cooldown for all allies.
notes:
An evolution of the reactive armour concept, ablative nano-plating was initially designed as a prototype technology for self-repairing heat shields on aircraft repeatedly entering and exiting planetary atmospheres.

- This is a science fiction version of ablative heat shielding, which is used primarily on spacecraft. The earliest description of this technology is
found in Robert H. Goddard's 1920 letter to the Smithsonian Institution, hence the faction logo. Goddard pioneered much of the technology used in modern space
exploration, and a lot of his research was made possible through a grant from the Smithsonian Instiitution.
- In reality self-repairing materials exist, but ablative heat shields do not self-repair. Instead, they are made in a manner which allows them to sustain greatly 
decreased wear from atmospheric re-entry.

name: Ridgeway's Pride
desc:
Shooting enemies within 15m applies bleed to the target.

Repair 3-48% of your armor per second for every enemy that is bleeding within 15m.

Repair strength per number of bleeding enemies:

1:3% 2:6% 3:12% 4:24% 5:48%
notes:
"You can tell a lot about a man by the way he bleeds. Me? I bleed for my country, and nothing else." 

- General Antwon Ridgeway

name: Bloody Knuckles
desc:
Damaging an enemy with a grenade or striking an enemy with a melee attack, activates Seeing Red. 

Seeing Red grants +25% weapon damage and +100% melee damage. 

Seeing Red lasts 20 seconds and starts a 60 second cooldown after completion.

While in cooldown, damaging an enemy with a melee attack or hitting an enemy with the effect of a grenade will complete the cooldown instantly.

Editor's note: the 25% "weapon damage" buff actually gives total weapon damage.
notes: "A broken watch is still a good memento." 13

name: BTSU Datagloves
desc:
Grants 15% Hive skill haste per skill tier.

Detonating a hive refreshes your skill cooldowns and grants overcharge for 15s. 
If at Skill Tier 6, this effect also applies to all allies.

Allies recieving this effect are unable to benefit from it again for 120s
notes:
"Pop always told me 'son, you're only as good as the tools you work with.' So I chose to work with the organisation with the best tools." 
- Galveston

- Galveston is the endboss of Camp White Oak, and was in charge of keeping track of President Ellis
- In real life, judging from crow's flight distance, Camp White Oak is most likely Camp David. This camp is a Presidental retreat, which makes the fact that the 
President retreated here a clever pun.

name: Exodus Gloves
desc:
On Armor break, drop a Smoke Bomb at your feet, concealing you from enemies for 3s.
Cooldown: 40s
notes: "I'd like to make a withdrawal."

name: Overdogs
desc:
Amplifies Weapon Damage by 30% to the lowest ranking enemies currently in combat, as per the Tier hierarchy.

Tier 1: Hunter, Rogue, Leader, Tank, Shield, Heavy Weapons, RPG, Medic, Controller, Warhound, Marauder.
Tier 2: Support, Engineer, Bodyguard, Immobilizer, Bomber, Mini Tank, Drone Operator.
Tier 3: Any other enemy or skill proxy.
notes:
"Start from the bottom and make your way up."

Note: Tier 3 is lowest, tier 1 is highest.

name: Rugged Gauntlets
desc:
-50% Recoil Penalty when hip-firing.

-50% Recoil Penalty when blind-firing from cover.
notes:
"Can't say I invented this, but at least I had a hand in it." 
- Calvin McManus

name: Centurion Scabbard
desc:
Swapping weapons will give the following groups of bonuses one by one, in order:

1. +20% Rate of Fire, +20% Weapon Damage
2. +50% Magazine Size, +50% Reload Speed

The bonuses remain active for 12s or until the next weapon swap. 
Swapping to your sidearm will not trigger the next group of bonuses.
notes: "it's better ti have more than one gun in your holster."

name:
Dodge City
Gunslinger's Holster
desc:
While your pistol is holstered, gain a stacking buff every 0.3s, up to 100.

When you swap to it, your first shot consumes the buff and deals +10% damage per stack.  This deals headshot damage to anywhere you hit.

REQUIRES
Pistol Equipped
notes:
"Fast is fine, but accuracy is everything. In a gunfight... You need to take your time in a hurry." 

- Wyatt Earp

-Blueprint flavour text: Ed managed to put together this retro-modern holster blueprint using the parts we found, and some more modern gear.
- Dodge City is an administrative name for Ford County, Kansas. It is famous for being a "wild west" town in the past.
- Its seal features the Texas Longhorn Steer, a species of cattle that were brought to the town from Texas. In fact, the town was unintentionally created by a
rancher of these cattle.

name: Imperial Dynasty
desc:
While in combat, applies burn to the enemy closest to you within 20m.

Cooldown: 35s
notes:
"Symbols are important. Look at the phoenix you wear on your sleeve. The dragon bears a similar significance to me." 
- Faye Lau

- SPOILERS AHEAD
- The Lau (or Liu) family was the ruling family of the Han Dynasty in ancient China. The ancient Chinese were deeply superstitious and the founder of the Han 
Dynasty, Liu Bang, claimed that he was conceived after his mother dreamt of a dragon. This was a clever move considering that in ancient China,  dragons were a 
symbol of imperial power and represented the Emperor. 
- The dragon bears a significance to Faye because just like the Han rebellion in ancient China, she believes that she will wipe out The Division take control.

name: Shocker Punch
desc:
While "Shocker Punch" is equipped, the stun received by the agent from the Shock status effect will be reduced by 50%.

Using a shield will give 100% to melee damage.

Using St. Elmo's Engine with the holster will give 100% extra melee damage and will make the next melee attack apply shock to the target. 

Using all three items will offer all of the above mentioned bonuses and the Shock from the melee attack will have a 5m radius, starting from the first target. 

15 second cooldown.
notes: "After getting sucker punched at the mall, I came up with a shocking solution." Anonymous

name: Waveform
desc: Generate a stack of 3% skill damage on one of your skills every second, capping at 10 stacks. After 10 seconds at the cap the stacks transfer to your other skill. The process then repeats ad-infinitum
notes: Graphite layers are suffused throughout the holster kevlar weave, that when the wearer moves creates an inductive oscillating electromagnetic field. This field allows for remote charging of electrical devices.

name: Nimble Holster
desc: Receive repairs of 5% of your Total Armor for every 1m run in cover-to-cover. Damage taken while doing a cover-to-cover is healed over 10s.
notes: "Run for your life."

name: Sawyer's Kneepads
desc:
Cannot be staggered by explosions.

Increases total weapon damage by 3% each second you are not moving.
Stacks up to 10 until you start moving.  All stacks lost 10s after moving.
notes:
"When I lost my leg, I thought that was it for me. But I found new purpose. I rose, just like the phoenix on my patch. Now it's time for these to find new purpose."

- Odessa Sawyer

- There is a deep irony in the fact that Odessa lost her leg to a sniper, causing her kneepads to become an exotic with a talent that greatly benefits players who 
use sniper rifles.
- Stand Your Ground does NOT cause players to ignore mortars in the Morozova fight.

name: Acosta Kneepads
desc:
Vaulting grants +10% Movement Speed for 3s. 
After not moving for 5s, recieve +20% Movement Speed for 5s.
Being applied a Stutus Effect grants +10% Movement Speed for 5s.
Max Movement Speed bonus is 20%. The bonuses do not stack with Movement Speed bonuses from other sources.
-50% Movement Impairment penalty from Status Effects.
notes:
"They got they asses caught by the JTF, but they don't have my mad parkour skills."
- Eva Acosta

name: Blacklisters
desc: Shooting an enemy applies a mark. Only one mark can be active at a time. The mark disappears after 15s, or when the marked enemy dies. Recieve 600% Amplified Damage from the marked enemy. Deal 20% Amplified Damage to unmarked enemies. If only one enemy remains in combat, the mark disappears and cannot be reapplied.
notes: Screw that one guy in particular.

name:
NinjaBike 
Messenger Kneepads
desc: Performing a cover to cover or vaulting reloads your drawn weapon and grants +25% bonus arrmor for 5s.
notes:
"Parkour! Parkour!" 
- an over-enthusiastic NinjaBike messenger

- I've worked 2 messenger jobs so far and I haven't met one person who knows how to do parkour
- I haven't met anyone who wears kneepads, either.
- NinjaBike doesn't exist, but Ninjavan is a real company.
- The type of vault that players do in game is most likely a clunky version of a speed vault, modified due to carrying a heavy backpack.
